myth: HTTPS means the website is safe
truth: HTTPS only encrypts transit — a phishing site can have a valid TLS certificate (free from Let's Encrypt). HTTPS protects against MitM attacks, not malicious content. Always verify the domain, not just the padlock.

myth: Encryption and hashing are the same thing
truth: Encryption is reversible (with the key): AES, RSA. Hashing is one-way: SHA-256, bcrypt. Encryption is for confidentiality, hashing is for integrity verification and password storage.

myth: Firewalls alone can prevent all attacks
truth: Firewalls filter network traffic but can't prevent application-layer attacks (SQL injection, XSS) that arrive via allowed ports (80/443). Defense-in-depth requires WAF, input validation, authentication, and monitoring.
